title: The Embedding Layer
concept: An embedding layer is a learnable lookup table in a neural network that maps a word index directly to its dense vector representation.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary role of an embedding layer in text processing?

options.text: It deletes words from the dataset.
It maps word indices to dense vectors.
It converts text to speech audio.

exercises.explain: An embedding layer translates basic categorical identifiers (like word indices) into rich numerical representations (vectors) that neural networks can easily process.

cards.title: From IDs to Meanings
cards.body: Computers don't read words, they read numbers. But if we just give them simple ID numbers like 'word #342', they won't understand how words relate. How do we give these numbers rich, learnable meanings? We use a special building block called an embedding layer!

cards.title: What is an Embedding Layer?
cards.body: An embedding layer is a special neural network layer that acts as a lookup table. Instead of doing heavy math, it takes the ID number of a word (like index 42) and directly retrieves a list of numbers (a dense vector) that represents that word's meaning.

cards.title: The Digital Warehouse Map
cards.body: Imagine a massive warehouse where every item has a unique bin code, but finding items by code is slow. An embedding layer is like a smart digital map that automatically translates a bin number (the word index) directly into GPS coordinates (the dense vector) showing exactly where it sits in the warehouse!

cards.title: Embedding in Action
cards.body: If our vocabulary has words like "apple", "banana", and "robot", they might get indices 0, 1, and 2. When the word "banana" (index 1) passes through the embedding layer, the layer pulls out its custom vector, such as [0.25, -0.89, 0.41], which the AI uses to understand its meaning.

cards.title: Are they fixed?
cards.body: Many people think embedding layers are static, pre-made dictionaries. In reality, they are completely learnable! As the AI practices and learns, it updates this lookup table so that words with similar meanings naturally end up with similar vectors.

cards.title: Sparse vs. Dense Embedding
cards.body: Unlike old-school sparse representations, embedding layers look up compact, meaningful vectors.

right.text: A short, dense list of decimal numbers containing rich semantic values.

left.text: A giant list of zeros with a single '1' at the word's position. Huge and empty.

exercises.prompt: What does an embedding layer map together?
exercises.explain: The embedding layer converts simple, isolated word indices into rich, multi-dimensional dense vectors that capture meaning.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The vector values inside an embedding layer are static and can never change or improve during training.
exercises.explain: Embedding layers are learnable parameters. The vectors are updated during training to help the AI represent relationships between words better.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe how an embedding layer retrieves word representations.
exercises.sentence: An embedding layer acts as a learnable [blank] table that maps word indices to dense vectors.
exercises.explain: An embedding layer works like a lookup table: you give it a word's index number, and it retrieves the corresponding vector of numbers.

exercises.prompt: Identify the accurate description of an embedding layer.

options.text: An embedding layer is a tool that prints out raw text to the screen.
An embedding layer maps word indices directly to dense vectors using a learnable lookup table.
An embedding layer converts all vectors back into giant sparse one-hot arrays.

exercises.explain: AI model B correctly identifies the embedding layer as a learnable lookup table for mapping indices to dense representations, whereas the others confuse it with one-hot encoding or text generation.

exercises.prompt: How does an embedding layer convert a word index into a dense vector?

options.text: It maps a word index to a dense vector using a learnable lookup table.
It converts continuous numbers into sparse binary matrices.
It permanently freezes word meanings so they can never be updated.
It removes all word representations to save computer memory.

exercises.explain: The embedding layer acts as a learnable lookup table. It takes high-level word indices (like token 143) and instantly retrieves their corresponding dense vector representation from its internal weights, bypassing the need for huge, sparse one-hot matrix multiplications.
